rows.confidence_rationale: Direct exposure to industrial demand stated multiple times in the memo.
rows.summary_rationale: Riverbend's revenue is concentrated in cyclical industrial verticals; a 200 bps rate shock would compress margins below covenant thresholds.

evidence_quotes.quote: 85% of Riverbend's customers are in cyclical industrial sectors.
evidence_quotes.relevance: Establishes concentration risk to industrial demand.

evidence_quotes.quote: Floating-rate debt represents 70% of total liabilities.

rows.inference_chain: Industrial concentration plus floating-rate debt yields compounding stress under SOFR +200 bps.
rows.recommended_followup: Confirm interest-rate hedging coverage and review covenant headroom under stress.

rows.confidence_rationale: Mixed indicators; some hedging in place.
rows.summary_rationale: Cascadia carries moderate floating-rate exposure but maintains a 60% hedge book that mutes the impact.

evidence_quotes.quote: 60% of floating-rate debt is hedged via interest-rate swaps maturing in 2027.
Industrial customer mix is 45% of revenue.

rows.inference_chain: Hedging coverage offsets a meaningful portion of the rate sensitivity.

rows.confidence_rationale: Memo explicitly addresses the scenario with quantitative answers.
rows.summary_rationale: Northwind's services-heavy mix and fixed-rate funding make a 200 bps shock immaterial to profitability.

evidence_quotes.quote: 97% of debt is fixed-rate with weighted-average maturity of 6.4 years.

rows.inference_chain: Fixed-rate funding insulates Northwind from short-term rate volatility.
